title: Air-fryer chicken breasts
subtitle: Juicy breast meat with a quick 20-minute brine
excerpt: Chicken breasts brined in salted water for 20 minutes, then cooked in the air fryer at 200°C for 16 to 18 minutes. The brine prevents the dryness that air-fried breast is prone to. The result is considerably juicier than a straight seasoned-and-cook approach.
sourceNotes: Chicken breast cooked in an air fryer produces dry results when cooked without preparation because the high dry heat pulls moisture out faster than oven roasting. A short brine (salt dissolved in water) seasons the meat all the way through and helps it retain moisture under the high heat. This technique is widely used in American air-fryer cooking and transfers directly to UK home cooks.

recipe.makeAheadNotes: Brine up to 2 hours ahead. Pat dry before cooking.

content.text: Twenty minutes in salted water transforms the texture of air-fryer chicken breast. Skip the brine and the result is correctly cooked but noticeably dry. The brine does not make the chicken salty: it seasons it evenly and the salt helps the protein retain its moisture under the intense heat of the air fryer.

items.prepNote: dissolved in 500 ml cold water for the brine

content.text: Dissolve the {{sea-salt-fine}} in 500 ml cold water in a bowl. Submerge the {{chicken-breast}} in the brine and leave for 20 minutes. Remove and pat completely dry with kitchen paper. Preheat the air fryer to 200°C for 3 minutes. Toss the dried breasts with the {{vegetable-oil}}, {{smoked-paprika}}, {{garlic-powder}}, and {{black-pepper}}. Place in a single layer in the basket. Cook at 200°C for 8 minutes, flip, then cook for a further 8 to 10 minutes until the internal temperature reaches 74°C. Rest for 5 minutes before slicing: the resting redistributes the juices.

items.symptom: Breast dry and stringy despite the brine
items.fix: Use a thermometer. Pull the chicken at 74°C: the carry-over heat will bring it to 76°C during the rest. Every degree past 80°C makes breast meat noticeably tougher

items.symptom: Outside burnt before centre is cooked
items.cause: Very thick breast or temperature too high
items.fix: Butterfly very thick breasts (cut horizontally almost through and open flat) to even the thickness before cooking. This ensures the outside and inside finish at the same time

items.cause: Insufficient oil or basket not preheated
items.fix: Always spray or brush the basket before use and preheat for 3 minutes. A light spray on the chicken itself also prevents sticking

content.text: Air-fryer chicken breast is a faster, lower-effort alternative to pan-frying. The brine adds 20 minutes of passive time but zero active effort. Use in salads, grain bowls, wraps, or alongside roast vegetables. The dry rub here gives a mildly smoky flavour: swap the paprika and garlic for lemon zest and herbs for a lighter version.
